thesis: Pre-merger SPAC proxy for Agility Robotics, the first US-listed pure-play humanoid robotics company. The $2.5B pre-money equity merger includes $620M+ in gross proceeds ($420M trust + $200M PIPE at $10/share led by Foxconn). Agility's Digit v5 humanoid is already deployed at 9 customer facilities with 65,000+ hours of operation and $300M+ in multi-year binding orders. Backed by Amazon, NVIDIA, SoftBank, and Google DeepMind. High-risk, high-reward speculative play on the commercialization of humanoid labor — but the stock has already run 50%+ above the PIPE price, so discipline on entry is critical.

financials.revenue: Pre-revenue; $300M+ multi-year binding orders (not yet recognized)
financials.net_income: Negative; Agility burned ~$100M in cash in 2025 per preliminary filings
financials.cash: $420M trust + $200M PIPE = $620M gross proceeds before redemptions
financials.fcf: Negative pre-revenue; runway extended via merger proceeds

drivers: S-4 declared effective and shareholder vote approves the merger in Q4 2026
Digit deployments scale from 9 facilities to 50+ by end of 2027
Toyota / Mercado Libre / Amazon orders convert into recurring RaaS revenue
NVIDIA partnership (Halos safety stack) accelerates enterprise adoption
Humanoid robotics TAM captures premium multiples similar to early AI infrastructure plays

base_case.assumptions: Merger closes in Q4 2026 with modest redemptions. Agility begins recognizing material revenue in 2027 as RaaS contracts ramp, but profitability remains years away. Market values the company at a speculative-but-reasonable premium to the $2.5B pre-money valuation.

risks: High shareholder redemptions leave Agility with less cash than planned
Merger closes late or fails due to regulatory/SEC delays
Digit adoption stalls at pilot stage; customers do not expand deployments
Competition from Tesla Optimus, Figure AI, and Apptronik undercuts pricing
Cash burn exceeds expectations and forces dilutive capital raise post-merger

catalysts.event: Expected closing of Agility Robotics merger and NASDAQ listing under CCXI
S-4 registration statement declared effective and shareholder vote
New commercial deployment announcements and Toyota RAV4 plant expansion

key_risks: SPAC merger may not close or may close with heavy redemptions
Pre-revenue with high cash burn and long path to profitability
Customer concentration and pilot-to-deployment conversion risk
Competitive humanoid robotics landscape (Tesla, Figure, Apptronik)
Stock has already run well above the $10 trust/PIPE price, creating entry risk
